description: Figure 2 presents a scatter plot illustrating the dependence of the austenite lattice parameter on silicon and aluminum alloying in Fe-Ni-Cr-based austenitic steels. The y-axis displays the Austenite Lattice Parameter in nanometers (nm), ranging from approximately 0.358 to 0.360 nm, while the x-axis indicates Relative Alloying content in weight percent (wt pct) from 0 to 3. Three distinct data points are shown: the Base alloy (Fe-15Ni-11Cr) at 0 wt pct with a lattice parameter of approximately 0.3585 nm (circular symbol), the 2.5 Si alloy at 2.5 wt pct with approximately 0.3587 nm (triangular symbol), and the 2.5 Al alloy at 2.5 wt pct with approximately 0.3598 nm (square symbol). The plot demonstrates that 2.5 wt pct Al addition significantly increases the austenite lattice parameter by approximately 0.0013 nm relative to the base alloy, whereas 2.5 wt pct Si exerts only a minimal effect, increasing the parameter by roughly 0.0002 nm. This data supports the conclusion that Al substantially expands the austenite lattice while Si has negligible influence on lattice dimensions.
